name: pending-stub
description: Placeholder pending increment. Will be properly planned when increment 32 completes.
sprintGoal: Advance E13 testing infrastructure in kind: deploy Chaos Mesh and Wiremock, validate HA gate compliance across all E13 charts, and restore backlog hygiene by retiring 14 stale confirmed-complete stories.

stories.title: Create scripts/test/kind-smoke.sh: static-verifiable scaffold covering PLATFORM-001 through PLATFORM-004
stories.description: Create scripts/test/kind-smoke.sh that encodes all PLATFORM-001 through PLATFORM-004 acceptance criteria as executable test functions (cert-manager, sealed-secrets, Harbor, Keycloak). The script must define one test function per PLATFORM story, print a PASS/FAIL summary table, and pass shellcheck -S error with no warnings. The exact commands for each test come from the acceptance criteria in prd/increment-21-platform-foundations.json.
acceptanceCriteria: shellcheck -S error scripts/test/kind-smoke.sh exits 0
grep -c 'helm\|kubectl' scripts/test/kind-smoke.sh returns a value >= 4 (at least one invocation per PLATFORM-00x test)
grep -E 'PASS|FAIL' scripts/test/kind-smoke.sh exits 0 (PASS and FAIL literals appear in summary output logic)
stories.testPlan: shellcheck -S error scripts/test/kind-smoke.sh; grep -c 'helm\|kubectl' scripts/test/kind-smoke.sh; grep -E 'PASS|FAIL' scripts/test/kind-smoke.sh; show all output verbatim.

stories.title: Execute kind-smoke.sh against kind-sovereign-test and verify PLATFORM-001 through PLATFORM-004 print PASS
stories.description: Run scripts/test/kind-smoke.sh against the kind-sovereign-test cluster. The script must complete without errors and print PASS for PLATFORM-001 (cert-manager), PLATFORM-002 (sealed-secrets), PLATFORM-003 (Harbor), and PLATFORM-004 (Keycloak). Capture the full output verbatim as proof. If any chart is not yet deployed, document the exact failure and set passes:false.
acceptanceCriteria: bash scripts/test/kind-smoke.sh --context kind-sovereign-test 2>&1 exits 0 and output contains at least 4 lines matching 'PASS'
Full output of kind-smoke.sh is captured verbatim in the story proof (no assertion without output)
stories.testPlan: bash scripts/test/kind-smoke.sh --context kind-sovereign-test 2>&1 | tee /tmp/kind-smoke-output.txt; cat /tmp/kind-smoke-output.txt; show all output verbatim.

smart.notes: Achievable score 3: requires kind-sovereign-test running with PLATFORM-001 through PLATFORM-004 deployed (cert-manager, sealed-secrets, Harbor, Keycloak) — a heavy dependency chain beyond this story's own scope. Specific score 4: PASS-line format depends entirely on HA-005a implementation, which is not yet written. Measurable score 4: 'at least 4 lines matching PASS' is binary but tightly coupled to the output format HA-005a will produce. TimeBound score 4: the fallback clause ('If any chart is not yet deployed, document the exact failure and set passes:false') introduces an open-ended failure-documentation sub-task.

stories.title: Enhance platform/charts/chaos-mesh/: add PDB, podAntiAffinity, probes, and resource limits
stories.description: Update the existing platform/charts/chaos-mesh/ Helm chart to meet the sovereign HA standard. Add a PodDisruptionBudget template with minAvailable=1, podAntiAffinity on hostname for chaos-controller-manager, readinessProbe and livenessProbe on chaos-controller-manager, and resource requests/limits on all containers. Set replicaCount>=2 for chaos-controller-manager in values.yaml. This is a static-verifiable change — no running cluster required.
acceptanceCriteria: helm lint platform/charts/chaos-mesh/ exits 0 with '0 chart(s) failed'
helm template platform/charts/chaos-mesh/ | grep PodDisruptionBudget returns at least 1 match
helm template platform/charts/chaos-mesh/ | grep podAntiAffinity returns at least 1 match
grep replicaCount platform/charts/chaos-mesh/values.yaml shows a value >= 2
stories.testPlan: helm lint platform/charts/chaos-mesh/; helm template platform/charts/chaos-mesh/ | grep PodDisruptionBudget; helm template platform/charts/chaos-mesh/ | grep podAntiAffinity; grep replicaCount platform/charts/chaos-mesh/values.yaml; show all output verbatim.

stories.title: Deploy platform/charts/chaos-mesh/ to kind-sovereign-test and run a pod failure experiment
stories.description: Install platform/charts/chaos-mesh/ into kind-sovereign-test in namespace chaos-testing. Create kind/fixtures/podchaos-test.yaml PodChaos experiment fixture targeting a test pod. Apply the fixture and verify the experiment reaches phase=Finished within 120 seconds and the pod recovers. This validates runtime behavior beyond static lint.
acceptanceCriteria: helm install chaos-mesh platform/charts/chaos-mesh/ --kube-context kind-sovereign-test --namespace chaos-testing --create-namespace --wait --timeout 3m exits 0
kubectl get pods -n chaos-testing --context kind-sovereign-test shows all pods Running
kubectl apply -f kind/fixtures/podchaos-test.yaml --context kind-sovereign-test exits 0 and kubectl get podchaos -n chaos-testing --context kind-sovereign-test shows phase=Finished within 120 seconds
kind/fixtures/podchaos-test.yaml exists and is valid YAML
stories.testPlan: helm install chaos-mesh platform/charts/chaos-mesh/ --kube-context kind-sovereign-test --namespace chaos-testing --create-namespace --wait --timeout 3m && kubectl get pods -n chaos-testing --context kind-sovereign-test && kubectl apply -f kind/fixtures/podchaos-test.yaml --context kind-sovereign-test && sleep 30 && kubectl get podchaos -n chaos-testing --context kind-sovereign-test; show all output verbatim.
stories.blocker: AC1-AC3 require kind-sovereign-test running with Chaos Mesh CRDs installed. No kind cluster is available in this execution environment. AC4 is complete: kind/fixtures/podchaos-test.yaml created and validated as YAML.

smart.notes: Specific score 4: the PodChaos fixture (kind/fixtures/podchaos-test.yaml) does not specify which test pod to target — the target selector is undefined. Measurable score 4: 'phase=Finished within 120 seconds' requires polling rather than a single deterministic command; exact polling mechanism is unspecified. Achievable score 4: requires kind-sovereign-test running with KIND-001a and KIND-001b accepted (both confirmed in sprints 31 and 32). TimeBound score 4: the 120-second polling window is empirical and the pod-recovery assertion after experiment completion is not explicitly capped.

stories.title: All E13 Helm charts (Selenium Grid, k6, Chaos Mesh, MailHog, Wiremock) pass HA gate
stories.description: Apply the platform HA standard to all five testing infrastructure charts. Each chart must have: replicaCount>=2 (where applicable), a PodDisruptionBudget with minAvailable=1, and podAntiAffinity on hostname. Run helm lint and helm template | grep PodDisruptionBudget for each chart. Single-instance-only components (MailHog, Wiremock) require at least a PDB.
acceptanceCriteria: helm lint platform/charts/selenium-grid/ exits 0
helm template platform/charts/selenium-grid/ | grep PodDisruptionBudget returns at least one match
helm lint platform/charts/chaos-mesh/ exits 0
helm template platform/charts/chaos-mesh/ | grep PodDisruptionBudget returns at least one match
helm lint platform/charts/mailhog/ exits 0
helm lint platform/charts/wiremock/ exits 0
stories.testPlan: for chart in selenium-grid chaos-mesh mailhog wiremock k6-operator; do echo "=== $chart ==="; helm lint platform/charts/$chart/; helm template platform/charts/$chart/ | grep -c PodDisruptionBudget; done — show all output verbatim.

smart.notes: Specific score 3: k6-operator appears in description and testPlan but has zero ACs — its HA compliance is unverified. Wiremock and chaos-mesh have only helm-lint ACs (no PDB or podAntiAffinity assertions), yet the description promises full HA treatment. Additionally, TEST-004a and TEST-005a (also in this sprint) already apply HA standard to chaos-mesh and wiremock respectively — TEST-006 does not list them as dependencies, so it is unclear whether this story performs implementation for those charts (duplicate work) or relies on the sibling stories (undeclared dependency). Measurable score 3: k6-operator is entirely absent from ACs despite being named in the title. TimeBound score 3: scope overlap with TEST-004a and TEST-005a makes the true implementation surface area ambiguous — it is unclear which charts this story is responsible for implementing vs only validating.

stories.title: Enhance platform/charts/wiremock/: add PDB, podAntiAffinity, probes, and resource limits
stories.description: Update the existing platform/charts/wiremock/ Helm chart to meet the sovereign HA standard. Add a PodDisruptionBudget template with minAvailable=1, podAntiAffinity on hostname, readinessProbe and livenessProbe, and resource requests/limits on all containers. Set replicaCount>=2 in values.yaml. This is a static-verifiable change — no running cluster required.
acceptanceCriteria: helm lint platform/charts/wiremock/ exits 0 with '0 chart(s) failed'
helm template platform/charts/wiremock/ | grep PodDisruptionBudget returns at least 1 match
helm template platform/charts/wiremock/ | grep podAntiAffinity returns at least 1 match
grep replicaCount platform/charts/wiremock/values.yaml shows a value >= 2
stories.testPlan: helm lint platform/charts/wiremock/; helm template platform/charts/wiremock/ | grep PodDisruptionBudget; helm template platform/charts/wiremock/ | grep podAntiAffinity; grep replicaCount platform/charts/wiremock/values.yaml; show all output verbatim.

stories.title: Deploy platform/charts/wiremock/ to kind-sovereign-test and verify admin REST API accepts stub mappings
stories.description: Install platform/charts/wiremock/ into kind-sovereign-test in namespace wiremock-test. Port-forward the Wiremock service and verify the admin API accepts a stub mapping POST to /__admin/mappings (expect HTTP 201) and that a matching GET to the mapped URL returns the configured response body. This validates runtime behavior beyond static lint.
acceptanceCriteria: helm install wiremock platform/charts/wiremock/ --kube-context kind-sovereign-test --namespace wiremock-test --create-namespace --wait --timeout 3m exits 0
kubectl get pods -n wiremock-test --context kind-sovereign-test shows all pods Running

kubectl port-forward -n wiremock-test svc/wiremock 8080:8080 --context kind-sovereign-test & sleep 2 && curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' -X POST http://localhost:8080/__admin/mappings -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"request":{"method":"GET","url":"/api/test"},"response":{"status":200,"body":"ok"}}' returns 201
curl -s http://localhost:8080/api/test returns 'ok'
stories.testPlan: helm install wiremock platform/charts/wiremock/ --kube-context kind-sovereign-test --namespace wiremock-test --create-namespace --wait --timeout 3m && kubectl get pods -n wiremock-test --context kind-sovereign-test && kubectl port-forward -n wiremock-test svc/wiremock 8080:8080 --context kind-sovereign-test & sleep 2 && curl -X POST http://localhost:8080/__admin/mappings -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '...' && curl http://localhost:8080/api/test; show all output verbatim.
stories.blocker: All ACs require kind-sovereign-test running with Wiremock deployed. No kind cluster is available in this execution environment. No static artifacts to create — story is entirely runtime verification.

smart.notes: Specific score 4: the port-forward background-process pattern ('& sleep 2 &&') is fragile — no specification of what happens if port-forward fails before curl runs. Measurable score 4: AC3 (curl returns 201) depends on the background port-forward being ready after exactly 2 seconds, which is non-deterministic in CI environments. Achievable score 4: requires kind-sovereign-test running with KIND-001a and KIND-001b accepted.

stories.title: Backlog hygiene: mark 14 confirmed-complete stories as status:complete and refresh stale dependency blocks
stories.description: The backlog contains 14 stories with readinessNote indicating 'COMPLETED IN SPRINT XX: passes:true, reviewed:true', but their status field is still 'open'. The grooming ceremony flags them but does not update lifecycle status. This means the planning ceremony sees them as open work and can accidentally re-pull already-delivered stories. The 14 stories are: CEREMONY-009, CEREMONY-007, CEREMONY-006, RESTRUCTURE-001c, CEREMONY-003, CEREMONY-004, QUALITY-005, HA-006, DEVEX-007a, DEVEX-007b, HA-007, CEREMONY-010, CEREMONY-011, QUALITY-005r. Additionally, 6 stories carry stale BLOCKED readinessNotes referencing 'KIND-001b has passes=false' — KIND-001b was accepted in increment 32. Update those readinessNotes to reflect the current state.
acceptanceCriteria: python3 -c "import json; b=json.load(open('prd/backlog.json')); stale=[s['id'] for s in b['stories'] if 'COMPLETED IN SPRINT' in s.get('readinessNote','') and s.get('status','open')!='complete']; assert stale==[], f'still open: {stale}'; print('PASS: all confirmed-complete stories marked complete')" exits 0 and prints PASS

python3 -c "import json; b=json.load(open('prd/backlog.json')); stale=[s['id'] for s in b['stories'] if 'KIND-001b has passes=false' in s.get('readinessNote','')]; print(f'stale KIND-001b blocks: {stale}')" prints an empty list
python3 -m py_compile scripts/ralph/ceremonies.py exits 0
stories.testPlan: Run AC1 one-liner verbatim and show output. Run AC2 one-liner verbatim and show output. Run AC3 py_compile and show output.
